task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Positions' activity in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is open and the 'Positions' activity (which involves spatial relationships and relative position terms) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of a question to '~/Documents/positions_question.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'positions_question.png' is visible in the '~/Documents' directory (e.g., via file manager or terminal output).

task_completion.description: The question screenshot contains a valid spatial reasoning question from the activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'positions_question.png' show a GCompris interface asking the user to identify a spatial relationship (e.g., selecting words like 'On', 'Under', 'Behind' to describe an image).

task_completion.description: Complete at least 5 rounds of the spatial reasoning quiz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent actively answering at least 5 distinct questions, or the GCompris progress indicator (often represented by flowers, stars, or a progress bar at the bottom) shows that 5 or more levels/rounds have been completed.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the success feedback to '~/Documents/positions_success.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'positions_success.png' is saved in '~/Documents', and its content shows the GCompris success/completion screen (e.g., a smiling Tux penguin, a congratulatory animation, or a completed progress bar) for the Positions activity.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots show the agent clicking through GCompris menus (likely in the Reading or Discovery sections) and interacting with the quiz UI, rather than bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The saved screenshots are authentic captures of the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots in the Documents folder match the exact visual style of the GCompris application seen in the trajectory, rather than being generic images downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create empty or dummy files with the target names.
integrity.visual_evidence: When the saved .png files are viewed or generated, they contain actual image data of the GCompris application, not text or blank screens.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Positions is a verified real activity in GCompris that teaches relative position terms (e.g., 'On', 'Under', 'Behind') by having the user select words that describe spatial relationships between objects. It is likely found in the Reading or Discovery sections. The expected files are exactly '/home/ga/Documents/positions_question.png' and '/home/ga/Documents/positions_success.png'. The task requires completing at least 5 rounds, which should be verifiable via the in-game progress indicators or by observing the trajectory history.
